mentions.note: Tim Cook legacy: 1,900% stock gain, 43% buybacks, zero compromise on quality or customer satisfaction. Rare consumer enterprise that achieved scale and loyalty. Successor must maintain customer-first culture.

Private credit fund redemption fears proved overblown; employees stepped in voluntarily to buy funds. Software stocks rebounded, easing Anthropic competition narrative. Stock bounced from low 100s to 133.
Proven optical play with NVIDIA endorsement. Preferred alternative to POET for exposure to AI data center optical buildout.

AI/Anthropic competition was false narrative; actually a tailwind for cybersecurity budgets. KeyBank upgrade validates. Stock rallied 4% today despite market down; first sign of comeback from purgatory.
Excellent Q1: Revenue +29% YoY, EPS beat $0.26, orders +87%, backlog $210B+. Conservative full-year guidance despite beat. Stock fell 5.6% on guidance realism; attractive entry on further weakness.
Home builders well-positioned with Fed policy support. D.R. Horton earnings strong. Caller Sandy down 20% should buy more; trust management team. Toll also performing well.
Buy at 23-24 level. CEO Michelle Gass executing well; stock has been right on thesis.
Proven optical play with NVIDIA endorsement. Preferred alternative to POET for exposure to AI data center optical buildout.

Caller noted triple-bagger gains from data center buildout fundamentals. Cramer acknowledges CEO Matt Murphy is excellent and stock fundamentals strong, though recently removed from bullpen due to portfolio constraints.

Copilot concerns overdone; Azure cloud infrastructure robust, Windows franchise second to none. Citi confirms strong reseller demand and terrific Azure results. Stock up $6 to 424 after recovery bounce started late March.

Optical interposer play for AI data centers with interesting partnerships (Lightwave, Lumentum). Up 175% in 12mo, 72% this month; trading 150x 2026E sales. Still in proving stage. Wait for pullback; prefer Lumentum or Coherent.

Strong Q1: Revenue +9-10% organic, op profit +14%, FCF +65%. Raised FY sales and EPS guidance. Pratt Whitney engines +23%, munitions +40%. Coyote counter-UAS ramping. Stock down 4.4% despite beat; attractive entry.
CEO Steve Hemsley returned in May 2025 and delivering upside surprises as expected. Company reported first of many upside beats. Stock up nearly 7%; believe it can return to former greatness.
